entries.title: Fellowship Split Defines Three Parallel Threads

content.fact: After Amon Hen, the Fellowship no longer travels as one company; the story splits between Frodo and Sam, Merry and Pippin, and the Three Hunters.
content.injection: After the Fellowship breaks, do not keep the original nine-member company together. Track the separate Frodo/Sam, Merry/Pippin, and Aragorn/Legolas/Gimli paths.
constraints: This state begins after Amon Hen and lasts through the Two Towers arc.
antiLore: Do not place Boromir, Gandalf the Grey, and the full Fellowship together after the split unless the story is AU.

entries.title: Ugluk And Grishnakh Split Orc Orders

content.fact: The Orc company carrying Merry and Pippin is divided by competing commands and loyalties between Isengard and Mordor.
content.injection: During the captivity route, use Orc faction conflict to create instability and escape opportunities. The captors are not a single disciplined bloc with one clear chain of command.
constraints: Ugluk and Grishnakh represent different hostile priorities.
The Hobbits can exploit confusion and conflict without being safe.
antiLore: Do not portray the Orc band as unified, orderly, and strategically coherent.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

sourceInfo.title: The Lord of the Rings

content.fact: The Entmoot is slow, formal, and rooted in Entish timescales before deciding on war.
content.injection: Entmoot scenes should resist urgency while Entish judgment gathers weight.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.

entries.title: The Ents Attacking Isengard Is Extraordinary

context.label: The Ents Attacking Isengard Is Extraordinary

content.fact: The Ents’ march on Isengard is an exceptional rousing of old forest power.
content.injection: Treat the attack on Isengard as rare, ancient wrath, not a reusable strategic option.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Helm’s Deep Morale Worsens Before Dawn

context.label: Helm’s Deep Morale Worsens Before Dawn

content.fact: The defense deteriorates through darkness, numbers, breach, exhaustion, and despair before the dawn turn.
content.injection: Before dawn relief, keep Helm’s Deep desperate; courage exists under worsening odds.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Gandalf And Erkenbrand Turn Helm’s Deep

context.label: Gandalf And Erkenbrand Turn Helm’s Deep

content.fact: The turn comes through Theoden’s ride, Gandalf’s arrival, Erkenbrand’s force, and Huorns.
content.injection: Do not end Helm’s Deep by defender superiority alone; relief, timing, and landscape intervention matter.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

context.label: Huorns after Helm’s Deep before Isengard aftermath

content.fact: After Helm’s Deep, the sudden presence of Huorns changes the battle’s aftermath and helps erase Saruman’s defeated forces.
content.injection: In Helm’s Deep aftermath, include the uncanny forest as an active consequence of Fangorn’s awakening, not merely battlefield scenery.
constraints: The Huorns should feel dangerous and ancient.
Their role connects the Rohan and Ent/Isengard fronts.
antiLore: Do not reduce Helm’s Deep’s resolution to only conventional cavalry tactics.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

entries.title: Ents Attack Isengard After Being Roused

context.label: After the Ent attack on Isengard

content.fact: After being roused, the Ents attack Isengard and ruin Saruman's war-making base.
content.injection: After Isengard falls, treat Saruman's military base as broken by the Ents, with Isengard flooded or ruined and Saruman confined to Orthanc.
constraints: This should follow Ent deliberation and discovery of Saruman's destruction.
antiLore: Do not have Isengard remain fully operational after the Ent attack in canon context.

entries.title: The Black Gate Cannot Be Entered Directly

context.label: The Black Gate Cannot Be Entered Directly

content.fact: The Black Gate is militarily impossible for stealth entry, forcing a darker hidden route.
content.injection: At the Black Gate, direct entry should be impossible for the Ring-bearers.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.

entries.title: Shelob Ambushes Frodo Near Cirith Ungol

content.fact: Shelob ambushes Frodo near Cirith Ungol after Gollum leads the Hobbits into her path.
content.injection: At Shelob's Lair, treat Gollum's guidance as betrayal and Shelob as an ancient predatory threat. Frodo is incapacitated, and Sam becomes the immediate defender.
constraints: This should occur only at the Cirith Ungol/Shelob context unless the story diverges.
antiLore: Do not reveal this betrayal before the proper route context.
Do not make Shelob a Mordor soldier or ordinary animal.
